_meta.source: Bing AI grounding report 2026-05-11 identified 270+ citations across regulator/registry library records. This file pins the canonical anchor record per jurisdiction; the broader corpus (110+ historical annual reports) is discoverable via library search.
_meta.comparison_frame_notes: Each comparison_frame.dd_fields is an HTML fragment with inline citation hyperlinks to library records. Render with Astro set:html. Inline citations are MANDATORY per editorial policy: every claim about ART, clinics, or registries must link to a primary source.
_meta.mandate_architecture_taxonomy_note: The 8-type mandate_architecture taxonomy is derived from internal research at ~/iCode/research-drafts/art-registry-comparison/art-registry.json but the broader 36-axis completeness scoring is deliberately NOT surfaced publicly because not all axes carry equal weight; equal-weighted scoring would mislead patient readers. Keep the taxonomy field (categorical) and drop any aggregate score (weighted).

codes_of_practice.title: HFEA Code of Practice
codes_of_practice.summary: The most detailed national code globally. Statutorily binding under the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Acts of 1990 and 2008. Covers staff qualifications, lab quality, embryo storage, donor anonymity, age limits, and clinic data submission requirements.

codes_of_practice.issuer: Reproductive Technology Accreditation Committee (RTAC), Fertility Society of Australia & New Zealand
codes_of_practice.title: RTAC Code of Practice for Reproductive Technology Units
codes_of_practice.summary: Clinic-accreditation framework rather than statutory law. Governs staff competence, lab QC, ethical conduct, and consumer disclosure. Clinics that fail RTAC accreditation lose access to Medicare rebates.

codes_of_practice.title: Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act
codes_of_practice.summary: Foundational UK statute that established the HFEA and the entire regulated-treatment framework. Subsequently amended in 2008.

codes_of_practice.title: Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act
codes_of_practice.summary: Major amendment to the 1990 Act. Expanded the regulated activities, clarified parenthood for same-sex couples, and refined storage and consent rules.

codes_of_practice.issuer: MRC + Royal College of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists
codes_of_practice.title: Final report of the Medical Research Council/Royal College of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists multi-centre trial
codes_of_practice.summary: Landmark UK self-audit of early ART. Cited as a methodological precedent for transparent multi-centre outcome reporting.

national_registries.title: Q-IVF Annual Report 2025: Fertility Treatments in Sweden (Treatment Year 2023)
national_registries.strengths: Cumulative patient-level outcomes and drop-out reporting; one of the few registries that surfaces these patient-facing numbers.
national_registries.limits: Limited demographic detail; no per-clinic public dashboards.

national_registries.operator: Japan Society of Obstetrics and Gynecology, Ethics Committee
national_registries.title: Assisted Reproductive Technology in Japan: A Summary Report for 2023
national_registries.strengths: Largest-volume ART registry globally by cycle count. Detailed age stratification.
national_registries.limits: Limited cumulative patient outcomes; very limited cost transparency.

national_registries.operator: National Perinatal Epidemiology and Statistics Unit, UNSW Sydney
national_registries.title: Assisted Reproductive Technology in Australia and New Zealand 2023
national_registries.strengths: Detailed per-cycle initiation reporting; strong perinatal outcome linkage.
national_registries.limits: Per-clinic data is aggregated; no public per-clinic dashboards.

national_registries.title: CARTR Plus 2025 Annual Report (2024 Data)
national_registries.strengths: Voluntary but near-complete clinic participation. Good age stratification.
national_registries.limits: No statutory mandate; participation depends on clinic willingness.

national_registries.title: DIR Jahrbuch 2024 (Deutsches IVF-Register Annual Report 2024)
national_registries.strengths: Long historical continuity since the 1990s. Detailed lab-quality metrics.
national_registries.limits: Reporting in German; less accessible to global readers.

national_registries.title: RLA 2021: Registro Latinoamericano de Reproduccion Asistida
national_registries.limits: Reporting cadence is irregular; some countries under-report.

national_registries.operator: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
national_registries.title: 2019 Assisted Reproductive Technology Fertility Clinic and National Summary Report
national_registries.strengths: Mandated under the Fertility Clinic Success Rate and Certification Act of 1992. Per-clinic detail available via CDC website.
national_registries.limits: Consistently 2-3 years lagged. No cumulative-LBR-per-patient surfacing. SART reports overlap but use different methodology.

national_registries.operator: European Society of Human Reproduction and Embryology, European IVF Monitoring Consortium
national_registries.title: Assisted Reproductive Technology in Europe, 2013
national_registries.limits: Substantial lag between treatment year and publication; this entry is the most recent EIM report in our corpus.

national_registries.operator: International Committee for Monitoring Assisted Reproductive Technologies
national_registries.title: ICMART World Report on Assisted Reproductive Technology (most recent)
national_registries.strengths: Cross-national aggregate methodology; useful for global trend comparisons.

comparison_frames.governance: Statutory regulator under the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 1990 and the 2008 amendment. HFEA both licenses clinics under the Code of Practice and operates the register.
comparison_frames.submission: Mandatory for every licensed UK clinic that creates, stores, or transfers embryos, under the 1990 Act.
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes per clinic plus stored patient identifiers. Research-linked analyses (such as the JAMA HFEA cumulative analysis) can derive patient-level cumulative outcomes.
comparison_frames.publishes: Clinic-by-clinic searchable success rates with comparison tools, multi-year national trends, multiple-birth rates.
comparison_frames.omits: Patient-level cumulative outcomes from public tools, drop-out, journey length from first consultation to live birth, out-of-pocket cost.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Both NHS-funded and private self-pay cycles.
comparison_frames.coverage: Universal across licensed UK clinics under the 1990 Act.

comparison_frames.governance: Federal agency reporting under the Fertility Clinic Success Rate and Certification Act of 1992 (FCSRCA), the mandate documented in the CDC ART National Summary. Data validated through medical-director attestation and Westat verification visits, but no clinical-practice regulation accompanies the data mandate.
comparison_frames.submission: Mandatory clinic reporting under FCSRCA. Non-reporting clinics are publicly named in the CDC report.
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes per clinic, patient and cycle characteristics, age band (CDC NSS methodology).
comparison_frames.publishes: Clinic-by-clinic success rates via an online tool, national aggregate summaries.
comparison_frames.omits: Patient-level cumulative outcomes, drop-out, out-of-pocket cost, longitudinal patient journey.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Predominantly private self-pay and employer-insured cycles, with state-mandate insurance pockets (NY, IL, MA, others). Captures all SART-clinic cycles regardless of payer.
comparison_frames.coverage: Near-universal among US ART clinics (approximately 95 to 99 percent), per the CDC NSS.
comparison_frames.publication_lag: 2 to 3 years per the CDC publication schedule.

comparison_frames.governance: Society-operated registry (NPESU at UNSW Sydney for FSANZ), documented in the ANZARD 2023 annual report. Submission is a condition of RTAC accreditation, which clinics need for Medicare rebate eligibility.
comparison_frames.submission: Effectively mandatory via RTAC accreditation tied to Medicare rebates.
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes with patient linkage across successive cycles, including perinatal outcomes via NPESU data linkage (ANZARD 2023).
comparison_frames.publishes: National aggregate report annually, patient-linked cumulative live birth rates by age, multiple-birth rates, perinatal outcomes.
comparison_frames.omits: Per-clinic public dashboards (clinic-level data is aggregated rather than searchable), out-of-pocket cost, diagnostic indication detail.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Medicare-rebated and private self-pay cycles across Australia; mixed funding in New Zealand.
comparison_frames.coverage: Near-universal across RTAC-accredited clinics in Australia and New Zealand.

comparison_frames.governance: Voluntary national quality registry operated by Sveriges IVF-register on behalf of Swedish fertility clinics, per the Q-IVF 2025 annual report.
comparison_frames.submission: Voluntary, but participation is broad in the context of Sweden's single-payer health system (Q-IVF 2025).
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes plus patient-linked cumulative outcomes across multiple cycles; drop-out tracking (Q-IVF 2025).
comparison_frames.publishes: Annual report with cumulative live birth rates per patient by age band, attrition, multiple-birth rates.
comparison_frames.omits: Per-clinic public dashboards, out-of-pocket cost analyses, detailed diagnostic indication.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Predominantly publicly funded cycles under Swedish regional health authorities, with some private self-pay.
comparison_frames.coverage: Broad across Swedish ART clinics in a single-payer system (Q-IVF 2025).

comparison_frames.governance: Voluntary registry operated by Deutsches IVF-Register e.V., a non-profit registered association of German fertility centres (DIR Jahrbuch 2024).
comparison_frames.submission: Voluntary membership. The 2024 Jahrbuch covers 139 member centres.
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes, embryo culture method, freeze-all use, transfer policy, perinatal outcomes (DIR Jahrbuch 2024).
comparison_frames.publishes: Annual Jahrbuch (yearbook) and a patient-facing Sonderausgabe summarising national aggregate data.
comparison_frames.omits: Patient-level cumulative outcomes, drop-out, out-of-pocket cost, English-language reporting.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Statutory health insurance (which partially covers ART for married couples meeting criteria) plus private self-pay.
comparison_frames.coverage: 139 member centres per the DIR Jahrbuch 2024; broad national presence but not universal.

comparison_frames.governance: Professional society registry maintained by the JSOG Ethics Committee, with reporting mandatory for JSOG member centres performing ART (JSOG 2023 summary).
comparison_frames.submission: Mandatory for JSOG member centres performing ART, though without statutory backing outside the society's own framework (JSOG 2023).
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes, detailed age stratification, frozen and fresh transfer cycles (JSOG 2023).
comparison_frames.publishes: Annual summary report with national aggregate cycle counts and per-cycle live birth rates.
comparison_frames.omits: Patient-level cumulative outcomes, drop-out across cycles, out-of-pocket cost, English-language detail.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Predominantly private self-pay before April 2022, then mostly public insurance after the 2022 national insurance reform extended ART coverage for women under 43.
comparison_frames.coverage: Broad across Japanese ART centres in a high-volume programme reporting over 450,000 cycles per year (JSOG 2023).

comparison_frames.governance: Voluntary registry operated by the Canadian Fertility and Andrology Society (CARTR Plus 2025 annual report). Operates outside Canada's federal Assisted Human Reproduction Act framework.
comparison_frames.submission: Voluntary; participation is broad among accredited Canadian clinics but not legally mandated (CARTR Plus 2025).
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes across Canadian fertility centres, age stratification, cycle type (CARTR Plus 2025).
comparison_frames.publishes: Annual national aggregate report with per-cycle live birth rates by age band.
comparison_frames.omits: Patient-level cumulative outcomes, drop-out, per-clinic public dashboards, out-of-pocket cost.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Predominantly private self-pay, with provincial pockets of public funding (Quebec 2010 to 2015, Ontario partial, Manitoba tax credit).
comparison_frames.coverage: Broad among accredited Canadian clinics; not universal (CARTR Plus 2025).

comparison_frames.governance: Voluntary regional federation of Latin American ART centres (RLA 2021 report). Operates outside any single national statute.
comparison_frames.submission: Voluntary. REDLARA reports membership of more than 90 percent of centres performing ART in the region (RLA 2021).
comparison_frames.collects: Cycle-level outcomes aggregated across 12-plus Latin American countries with varying national reporting frameworks (RLA 2021).
comparison_frames.publishes: Regional Registro Latinoamericano de Reproduccion Asistida reports.
comparison_frames.omits: Country-by-country comparability is limited; patient-level cumulative outcomes, per-clinic dashboards, out-of-pocket cost.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Predominantly private self-pay across Latin America, with public coverage pockets in Argentina, Uruguay, and selected Brazilian states.
comparison_frames.coverage: More than 90 percent of regional ART centres reporting through REDLARA membership (RLA 2021).
comparison_frames.publication_lag: Approximately 3 or more years; reporting cadence is irregular.

comparison_frames.governance: Society-operated meta-registry coordinated by ESHRE that aggregates submissions from 30-plus European national registries (ESHRE EIM 2013 report).
comparison_frames.submission: Voluntary national submission; EIM inherits whatever submission requirements each member country imposes domestically.
comparison_frames.collects: Aggregated national cycle-level data, ART cycle counts by country, single-embryo-transfer trends, multiple-birth rates (ESHRE EIM 2013).
comparison_frames.publishes: European IVF Monitoring reports in Human Reproduction Open and ESHRE publications.
comparison_frames.omits: Country-by-country methodological harmonisation is limited; per-clinic data, out-of-pocket cost, patient-level cumulative outcomes.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Inherits the funding-source mix of each contributing national registry; varies widely across Europe.
comparison_frames.coverage: Variable; depends on which national registries submit and how completely.
comparison_frames.publication_lag: Typically 4 to 6 years between treatment year and EIM publication.

comparison_frames.governance: Voluntary global meta-registry coordinated by ICMART that aggregates submissions from national registries worldwide (ICMART World Report).
comparison_frames.submission: Voluntary national submission; ICMART inherits each country's domestic reporting structure.
comparison_frames.collects: Global aggregate cycle counts and outcomes drawn from participating national registries (ICMART World Report).
comparison_frames.publishes: ICMART World Reports on Assisted Reproductive Technology.
comparison_frames.omits: Per-country methodological reconciliation; per-clinic data; patient-level cumulative outcomes; out-of-pocket cost.
comparison_frames.funding_capture: Inherits the funding-source mix of each contributing national registry.
comparison_frames.coverage: Variable; weighted toward countries with mature national registries.
